era: 1982-1986. Hardcore Punk. E=8 -- fast, aggressive, Capitol Hill-anchored. Independent.
members: Documentation limited -- Capitol Hill hardcore, 1982-1986 generation

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill hardcore 1982-1986. Neighborhood-specific hardcore with political character: more explicitly leftist, more queer-adjacent than suburban scenes. Community density as creative force.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill enters the engine in its hardcore phase at degree 274.

theory_nodes.concept: Four-year burn: 1982-1986. Short-run bands as hardcore documents. Vexed as a document of Capitol Hill at peak intensity. Short-lived bands have disproportionate influence relative to duration.
theory_nodes.description: The short run as document: four years, maximum energy, dissolution.

theory_nodes.concept: E=8 holds from 273. Hardcore streak: The Refuzors (273) to Vexed (274). Both E=8. The highest sustained energy in the S36 engine.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 holds. Seattle citywide to Capitol Hill. Hardcore generation peers. Refuzors = 40-year institution; Vexed = 4-year document. Same energy, different duration.
Seattle hardcore founding generation. Same early 1980s scene, same DIY infrastructure.
Energy drops from 8 to 5. Hardcore to lo-fi punk. Seattle to Olympia. K Records enters. Geographic and tonal shift.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8. Combined E=16. Vertical rise + crash paired with Capitol Hill hardcore. Cultural curiosity behavior from the orca; political engagement from the band.
